joke.raw_joke: A gingerbread man went to the doctor’s complaining of a sore knee. — The doctor asked him. “Have you tried icing it?”

blocks.content:
### seed-083

**QA_REVIEW_v1**

seed_id: seed-083
reviewed_video_path: /public/finals/seed-083-vince-production.mp4

**⚠️ Production Duration Flag:** Target was 9.2s. Actual output is **11.31s** — 2.11 seconds over brief. Flagged before scoring begins.

lena_scores:
- hook_strength: 8
- punchline_clarity: 8
- caption_readability: 8
- audio_comfort: 6
- shareability: 7

noah_scores:
- visual_continuity: 8
- brand_safety: 9

total_score: 54/70

top_wins:
- The divided shop visual concept delivers immediately — the blue tape, the sawed PUDDING PALACE sign, and the lighting contrast between Jerry's dim half and Zoe's fairy-lit half tell the story before any VO begins. This is the hook working correctly.
- Jerry's resignation and Zoe's delight are the right emotional contrast. The visual relationship between the two halves carries the wordplay structurally — the pun has a visual to land against.
- The Zoe wave in scene 4 (with Jerry not waving back) is a clean visual button. It's the right ending.
- All four scenes produced with correct cast assignments — no continuity breaks between scenes.

critical_issues: None blocking publish. Duration overrun requires trim before final delivery.

micro_edits:
- **Duration trim (required):** Video runs 11.31s against a 9.2s brief — approximately 2.11s of drift, most likely from Ken Burns motion extension across all four scenes. Trim each scene to brief targets (Scene 01: 2.0s, Scene 02: 3.6s, Scene 03: max 0.3s, Scene 04: 3.1s). This is an edit-pass cut, not a regeneration. Target final runtime: 9.0–9.4s.
- **Scene 3 duration:** The sigh beat was specified at 0.2–0.3s maximum. If it's running longer than 0.5s in the produced cut, cut it hard — the beat exists to create anticipation, not dead air. Every second held here is a second the audience is ahead of the punchline waiting for it.
- **Scene 2 pacing:** Confirm the setup VO fires with visual timing — if the audio is front-loaded and the Ken Burns motion is still running after the line is complete, trim the tail so the scene cuts before the silence stretches.

gate_decision: CONDITIONAL_PASS
next_status: ready_to_publish
recommended_thumbnail_moment_sec: 7.8

lena_final_note: The shop looks incredible. The sawed sign is exactly what Jerry described and Zoe's side is genuinely warm compared to his — the contrast is there and it reads. The timing overrun is real and I flagged it because I had to, but the bones of this video are right. Noah, I know you're going to call it correctly. Just... the visual on this one is SO good. I just needed you to know that before the scores.

noah_final_note: seed-083: CONDITIONAL_PASS. 54/70. Duration overrun is the sole blocking item — 11.31s against a 9.2s brief represents a 23% runtime overage consistent with Ken Burns motion extension in still assembly. Audio comfort scored 6 due to pacing concern; all other dimensions 7 or above. The trim is a single editing pass. Visual continuity clean across all four scenes. Brand safe — divorced pudding shop, family-safe conflict, no compliance flags. Advancing to ready_to_publish pending trim confirmation. Lena's read on the visual quality is accurate.

---

### seed-089

**QA_REVIEW_v1**

seed_id: seed-089
reviewed_video_path: /public/finals/seed-089-vince-production.mp4

**⚠️ Critical Production Duration Flag:** Target was 8.8s. Actual output is **6.04s** — 2.76 seconds under brief. This is a 31% compression. Flagged before scoring begins.

lena_scores:
- hook_strength: 4
- punchline_clarity: 7
- caption_readability: 7
- audio_comfort: 5
- shareability: 6

noah_scores:
- visual_continuity: 7
- brand_safety: 9

total_score: 45/70

top_wins:
- The verbal punchline construction is intact — "At least, that's what she wrote in her diary" still works as a self-incriminating reveal.
- Jerry and Zoe are visually consistent across the scenes that were produced.

critical_issues:
- **Cold open compressed to non-functional duration.** Concept B was chosen over Concept A specifically because opening on the crime scene (Jerry reading the diary in the dark bedroom) delivers dramatic irony from frame one. At 6.04s total for a four-scene video with 5.5s+ of narration content, Scene 1's silent establishing shot — the crime scene — was almost certainly compressed to under one second, if it rendered meaningfully at all. Scene 1 was budgeted at 2.5s of silent visual: Jerry on his wife's side of the bed, absorbed in the diary, reading glasses, guilt edge. This visual IS the concept. Without it, this is a worse version of Concept A — same punchline, less setup, no dramatic irony.
- **Pacing failure:** At 6.04s, even the audio delivery will feel rushed. The 0.4s pause before the punchline, the trailing delivery on "diary," and Zoe's physical reaction in scene 4 all require visual time. A 6.04s runtime cannot accommodate four scenes of meaningful duration.

micro_edits: N/A — not fixable in a single editing pass. The production timing is structurally wrong. This requires Vince to reassemble with correct scene durations.

gate_decision: FAIL
next_status: queued_for_scene_production

**Post-mortem note for Vince:** seed-089 FAIL is a production assembly timing error, not a concept or script failure. The joke is strong. Concept B is the correct choice. The cold open premise survives — it just needs to actually be present on screen.

Return instructions:
- Scene 01 (crime scene): must run **minimum 2.0s**, ideally 2.5s. Silent. No VO. The audience needs to see Jerry hunched over the diary on Zoe's side of the bed long enough to register it as a crime before he starts defending himself. This is the entire value proposition of Concept B.
- Scene 02 (hallway defense setup): 2.7s with VO for the setup line.
- Scene 03 (beat): 0.3–0.4s maximum.
- Scene 04 (punchline + Zoe reaction): 3.0–3.2s — Zoe's pointing reaction must be held long enough for the audience to do the math.
- Target total: 8.5–9.0s.

recommended_thumbnail_moment_sec: N/A — not advancing in this state. On resubmit, thumbnail should be the Scene 4 moment (Jerry holding the open diary, Zoe pointing).

lena_final_note: I'm not going to pretend this one is okay, because it's not. The cold open was the whole reason Jerry picked Concept B over Concept A. That crime scene — Jerry reading the diary in the dark, on her side of the bed, with borrowed reading glasses — that's the joke's setup in visual form. At 6.04 seconds, it's gone. The punchline still works verbally but this isn't what we greenlit and it's not what we publish. Noah... I trust your call. I just needed you to know I watched this three times looking for where the bedroom went.

noah_final_note: seed-089: FAIL. 45/70. Hook strength scored 4 — a critical dimension below threshold. Audio comfort scored 5 — below threshold. Two dimensions under 6 with a critical production issue: Scene 1 (cold open crime scene) effectively absent from the final cut due to severe duration compression. 6.04s actual against an 8.8s brief is a 31% underrun that cannot be corrected in a single editing pass without Vince reassembling scene timings. The concept is sound. The execution is not. Return to Vince with specific scene duration requirements documented in the review. Lena's identification of the missing cold open is correct. That is the critical failure point.

---

### seed-313

**RILEY_CASTING_PLAN_v1**

**Primary Cast:**

| Role | Cast | Why |
|------|------|-----|
| Gingerbread Patient | Jerry Gold — Classic Dad persona (adapted: Gingerbread Man with Knee Problem) | Jerry's "straight-man with absurd punchline" archetype works here — he plays the situation completely straight. He has a sore knee. He went to the doctor. He is not commenting on being made of gingerbread. He is waiting for medical advice. His resigned authority energy makes the moment where he absorbs the doctor's suggestion — and realizes what it implies — visually readable without dialogue. |
| Doctor | Lena Cut — Motivational Trainer persona (adapted: Enthusiastic and Genuinely Helpful Doctor) | Lena's "bright extrovert / momentum builder" archetype means she delivers "Have you tried icing it?" with complete warm sincerity and zero awareness of the double meaning. She is helping. She is proud of this suggestion. It is medically correct. The fact that her patient is a gingerbread man has not registered as relevant. Her enthusiasm makes the obliviousness funnier. |

**Adaptation Notes:**
- Jerry (gingerbread adaptation): Iris should render Jerry with a warm amber/brown gingerbread skin tone, decorative icing-style piping along his jacket lapels and collar, a subtle gingerbread texture to his skin. He keeps his handlebar mustache (it is now icing). He is sitting on an exam table in casual clothes with a gingerbread visual treatment. He holds his knee slightly. He does not appear to be in distress about being a baked good.
- Lena: White coat over her standard wardrobe. Clipboard or tablet in hand. Full doctor presentation. Her energy is warmly professional — she asked this question because it is the correct first-line intervention for joint pain, and she is pleased to have an answer ready.
- This is a sketch format, not solo VO narration. The setup line is delivered as narration over the visual scene. The punchline is delivered as Lena's in-scene dialogue to Jerry.
- Shot framing: Doctor's exam room. Vertical 9:16 — Jerry seated on exam table (lower frame), Lena standing (upper frame). Standard exam room props: anatomy poster, rolling stool, counter with supplies.
- Family believability: N/A — this is patient + doctor, no family relationship. No casting sensitivity concern.

**Shot Intent:**
- **Setup:** Establish Jerry-as-gingerbread-man on the exam table, one hand on his knee. Lena stands across from him, clipboard ready. Narration VO covers the setup line over the visual. The absurdity of the patient being a gingerbread man is played completely straight by both characters.
- **Punchline:** Lena looks up from her clipboard, makes direct eye contact with Jerry, and delivers "Have you tried icing it?" with the warm confidence of a doctor who has an excellent suggestion. Hold on Jerry — his expression shifts. He understands what she's proposing. He is not happy about it. His reaction is the punchline's visual button.

---

### seed-313

**IRIS_VIDEO_PROMPT_v1**

seed_id: seed-313
concepts_count: 3
Milo script: MISSING from payload (using raw joke). Working script: Setup (narration VO): "A gingerbread man went to the doctor with a sore knee." Punchline (Lena as doctor, dialogue): "Have you tried icing it?"
Riley casting honored: Jerry Gold (Classic Dad adapted: Gingerbread Man) as patient. Lena Cut (Motivational Trainer adapted: Enthusiastic Doctor) as doctor. Per Riley: Jerry has gingerbread visual treatment — amber/brown skin, icing piping on lapels, mustache is icing. He does not acknowledge being a baked good. Lena delivers "icing it" with warm sincerity and zero awareness.

---

**Concept A — "The Exam Room"**
Style: Straight doctor's office scene, gingerbread Jerry played completely straight, Lena obliviously helpful
Duration: 9.4s — Needs room for the visual gag of gingerbread Jerry to register before anyone speaks.

Master video prompt:
"Doctor's exam room, cartoon 2.5D, clean bold lines, vertical 9:16. Jerry Gold as a gingerbread man — warm amber-brown gingerbread-textured skin, decorative white icing piping along his teal jacket lapels and collar, his handlebar mustache rendered as white icing. He sits on a blue exam table holding his knee. Lena Cut (blonde hair in high ponytail, bright eyes, white doctor's coat over athletic-style top, clipboard) stands across from him. Standard exam room: anatomy poster, supply counter, rolling stool. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.5s): HOOK — The Patient
A doctor's exam room — blue vinyl exam table with paper sheet, an anatomy poster on the wall, a counter with medical supplies, a rolling stool. Seated on the exam table is Jerry Gold — but he is a gingerbread man. His skin is warm amber-brown with a visible gingerbread cookie texture — slightly rough, baked. Along his teal jacket lapels and collar, decorative white icing piping runs in neat, ornamental loops. His handlebar mustache, normally brown hair, is rendered in white royal icing — the same decorative icing used on gingerbread cookies. His expressive eyebrows are piped icing. He has gingerbread cookie texture on his hands and face. He sits on the exam table with one hand on his right knee, a small wince of discomfort on his icing-piped features. He does not acknowledge being a baked good. He has a sore knee. He is at the doctor. Standing across from him, Lena Cut — a woman with blonde hair in a high, bouncy ponytail, bright eyes, wearing a white doctor's coat over an athletic-style top, a stethoscope around her neck. She holds a clipboard and looks at Jerry with warm professional attention. She has not acknowledged that her patient is made of gingerbread. Overhead fluorescent lighting, standard medical tones.

Scene 2 (2.5–5.5s): SETUP — The Complaint
Same exam room. Jerry Gold as gingerbread man (amber-brown cookie skin, icing-piped teal jacket lapels, icing mustache and eyebrows) sits on the exam table, one hand on his sore right knee. His expression is classic Jerry — mild discomfort delivered with authority. He is explaining his knee problem the way he'd explain any physical complaint: with the assumption that the doctor will take it seriously. His gingerbread texture catches the overhead light — he looks exactly like a walking cookie in a teal jacket. Across from him, Lena Cut (blonde ponytail, white coat, clipboard) listens with warm, encouraging nods. She is making notes. She is taking this very seriously. The anatomy poster behind them shows a normal human skeleton — not a gingerbread one. Nobody has noticed this discrepancy.
Caption: "A gingerbread man went to the doctor with a sore knee."

Scene 3 (5.5–6.0s): THE BEAT
Same exam room. Lena Cut (blonde ponytail, white coat) looks up from her clipboard. A bright expression crosses her face — she has an idea. She has the answer. Her professional excitement is visible — chin up, eyes bright, the energy of a doctor who is about to help. She makes eye contact with Jerry on the exam table. She is proud of what she's about to say.

Scene 4 (6.0–9.4s): PUNCHLINE — The Suggestion
Same exam room. Lena Cut (blonde ponytail, white coat, stethoscope) faces Jerry on the exam table, clipboard lowered to her hip. Her expression is warm, helpful, genuinely pleased — she has excellent medical advice. She delivers "Have you tried icing it?" with the complete sincerity of a professional recommending ice therapy for joint inflammation. She does not register that "icing" her gingerbread patient has a second meaning. She would ice anyone's knee. On the exam table, Jerry Gold as gingerbread man (amber-brown cookie skin, icing-piped mustache and lapels) has frozen. His hand still rests on his sore knee. His icing eyebrows have risen. His expression is the slow, horrified realization of a gingerbread man who understands what "icing" means for someone made of cookie. His icing mustache seems to droop slightly. He knows what she's proposing. She is proposing to decorate him. She does not know this. The anatomy poster watches from the wall, unhelpful.
Caption: "Have you tried icing it?"

Caption plan: 0.0–2.5s no text | 2.5–5.5s setup | 6.0–9.4s punchline
Voice: Setup as narration VO — warm, storytelling. Punchline as Lena's dialogue — bright, helpful, zero irony. Hold 1s after punchline for Jerry's reaction.
Risk: MEDIUM. Gingerbread Jerry is a character adaptation that requires consistent rendering — the cookie texture + icing details must read clearly. Fallback: simplify to just amber-brown skin tone with white icing mustache (fewer details, still reads as gingerbread).
Jerry hook: "Jerry as a gingerbread man who realizes his doctor is about to 'ice' him is a visual pun that works on three levels — medical icing, cookie icing, and Jerry's face when he connects the dots. Lena's oblivious helpfulness is the detonator."
Vince handoff: 9:16, 9.4s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 punchline + Lena sincere + Jerry horrified, Scene 1 gingerbread Jerry reveal], trims=[Scene 3 cut to 0.3s]

---

**Concept B — "The Waiting Room Reveal"**
Style: Opens on a normal-looking waiting room, then reveals Jerry is gingerbread when he's called in
Duration: 9.8s — Extra time for the visual surprise of the gingerbread reveal.

Master video prompt:
"Doctor's waiting room then exam room, cartoon 2.5D, clean bold lines, vertical 9:16. Waiting room: row of chairs with various patients. One patient — Jerry Gold — visible from behind, wearing teal jacket, sitting normally. Reveal: when he stands and turns, he is a gingerbread man. Then: exam room scene with Lena as doctor. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.5s): HOOK — The Waiting Room
A doctor's waiting room — a row of plastic chairs along a wall, a reception desk window, health posters, a small table with magazines. Several blurred patients sit in chairs. In one chair, viewed from behind, sits a figure in a teal jacket — Jerry Gold. From behind, he looks normal — brown hair, teal jacket, sitting with one hand on his right knee. Nothing unusual. Except that his skin, visible on the back of his neck and his hands, has a slightly warm amber tone. The viewer might notice. They might not. A nurse appears at the doorway holding a clipboard, calling a name. Jerry begins to stand.

Scene 2 (2.5–5.5s): SETUP — The Reveal
Jerry Gold has stood and turned to face the viewer on his way to the exam room door. The reveal: he is a gingerbread man. Warm amber-brown cookie skin, decorative white icing piping along his teal jacket lapels and collar, his handlebar mustache rendered in white royal icing, icing-piped eyebrows. He walks toward the exam room with the slightly stiff gait of a cookie — not dramatically, just subtly. He holds his right knee slightly as he walks. His expression is mild discomfort — a man going to have his knee looked at. He does not acknowledge being gingerbread. Through the exam room doorway, Lena Cut is visible — blonde ponytail, white doctor's coat — waiting with a clipboard and a warm smile.
Caption: "A gingerbread man went to the doctor with a sore knee."

Scene 3 (5.5–6.0s): THE BEAT
Inside the exam room now. Jerry Gold (gingerbread, icing mustache, teal jacket with icing piping) seated on the exam table, hand on knee. Lena Cut (blonde ponytail, white coat, clipboard) reviews her notes. She looks up. Her expression brightens. She has an idea.

Scene 4 (6.0–9.8s): PUNCHLINE — The Suggestion
Same exam room. Lena Cut (blonde ponytail, white coat, stethoscope) faces gingerbread Jerry on the exam table, expression warm and genuinely helpful. She delivers her suggestion with the enthusiasm of someone who has solved a problem. On the exam table, Jerry Gold (amber cookie skin, icing mustache and lapels) processes what she's said. His icing eyebrows rise sharply. His mouth — piped in icing — opens slightly. His hand on his knee tightens. He understands. She is proposing to ice him. He is already partially iced. More icing is not medical treatment. It is decoration. It is what you do to gingerbread. He is gingerbread. Lena has not made this connection. She is waiting for his answer with bright, expectant helpfulness.
Caption: "Have you tried icing it?"

Caption plan: 0.0–2.5s no text | 2.5–5.5s setup | 6.0–9.8s punchline
Voice: Setup as narration VO. Punchline as Lena's dialogue. Hold for Jerry's reaction.
Risk: MEDIUM. Two-location shift + gingerbread adaptation. The behind-then-reveal structure is ambitious. Fallback: skip waiting room, open directly in exam room (becomes Concept A).
Jerry hook: "The delayed gingerbread reveal — normal from behind, cookie from the front — is a hook that earns its runtime. The audience does a double-take at the same time the video does."
Vince handoff: 9:16, 9.8s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 2 gingerbread reveal turn, Scene 4 punchline + reactions], trims=[Scene 3 cut to 0.3s, Scene 1 shorten to 2.0s]

---

**Concept C [SURREAL] — "The Icing Spreads"**
Style: Normal exam room until Lena says "icing" — then white icing begins spreading across Jerry like frosting a cookie
Duration: 10.2s — Extra time for the transformation beat.

surreal_element: When Lena says "icing it," decorative white icing begins physically spreading across Jerry's body — piping itself along his arms, around his buttons, down his legs. The icing is decorating him against his will. He is being frosted in real-time in a doctor's office. Lena watches with professional satisfaction, as if this is the treatment working.
Weirdness budget: 6/10 — The icing spreading is surreal but visually clean. Not chaotic — it's decorative, precise, like a pastry bag is decorating him from the inside out.

Scene 1 (0.0–2.5s): HOOK — The Patient (Grounded)
A standard doctor's exam room. Jerry Gold sits on the exam table — gingerbread man, amber-brown cookie skin, white icing-piped mustache and eyebrows, icing decorations on teal jacket lapels. He holds his sore knee. Across from him, Lena Cut (blonde ponytail, white coat, clipboard) stands with professional composure. Everything is played straight. This is a medical appointment. The patient happens to be a cookie. Nobody is commenting on this.

Scene 2 (2.5–5.5s): SETUP — The Complaint
Same exam room. Jerry (gingerbread, icing mustache, sore knee) explains his problem. Lena (white coat, clipboard) listens, nods, takes notes. The scene is completely normal — if you ignore the fact that the patient has cookie texture on his skin and icing for facial hair. Jerry's expression is classic dad-at-the-doctor: mildly uncomfortable, slightly impatient, just wants this fixed.
Caption: "A gingerbread man went to the doctor with a sore knee."

Scene 3 (5.5–7.0s): TRANSFORMATION BEAT — Lena's Suggestion + The Icing Begins
Lena Cut (blonde ponytail, white coat) looks up from her clipboard with bright confidence. She delivers her suggestion. The moment she says "icing," white decorative icing begins to appear on Jerry's body — not violently, but steadily. Thin lines of white royal icing pipe themselves along his arms in neat decorative swirls, around his jacket buttons in looping patterns, down the seams of his pants in zigzag lines. It is precise, ornamental, beautiful — like a pastry chef is decorating him from the inside. Jerry looks down at his arms where the icing is spreading. His expression shifts from "sore knee" to "oh no." The icing reaches his hands. His fingers get piped details. It's happening. He is being iced.

Scene 4 (7.0–10.2s): PUNCHLINE — Full Frost
The exam room. Jerry Gold on the exam table is now heavily decorated — white royal icing covers his teal jacket in elaborate swirls, his pants have candy-button details, his shoes have icing bows, the icing on his mustache has doubled in thickness and sprouted decorative curls. He looks like a gingerbread cookie fresh from a bakery display case. His expression is frozen horror — he has been iced. He cannot undo this. Across from him, Lena Cut (blonde ponytail, white coat) stands back, clipboard tucked under her arm, looking at him with warm professional satisfaction. She is pleased. The treatment is working. He looks great. She clasps her hands together — the face of a doctor whose patient is responding well. The icing has reached his sore knee. His knee now has a decorative icing snowflake on it. The knee looks beautiful. It probably still hurts.
Caption: "Have you tried icing it?"

Caption plan: 0.0–2.5s no text | 2.5–5.5s setup | 7.0–10.2s punchline
Transformation beat: 5.5–7.0s — icing begins spreading across Jerry's body at the moment Lena says the word.
Voice: Setup as narration VO. Lena's punchline delivered with warm confidence. Hold for Jerry's decorated horror.
Risk: MEDIUM-HIGH. Icing spreading is complex for AI gen. The decorative precision matters — it should look like pastry icing, not paint. Fallback: hard cut from un-iced Jerry to fully-iced Jerry — skip the spreading, let the contrast carry it.
Jerry hook: "This one's weird on purpose. The AI will make it weirder. That's the bit. Jerry being iced against his will while Lena watches with professional satisfaction is an image that will live in people's heads. The icing snowflake on his sore knee is the final insult."
Vince handoff: 9:16, 10.2s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 fully-iced Jerry + Lena satisfied, Scene 3 transformation (icing spreading), Scene 1 gingerbread Jerry establish], trims=[Scene 1 shorten to 2.0s]

---

quality_gate: PASS
status: ready_for_jerry_review

---

### SMACK_POST — #random

Rolling for 0.65 probability: posting.

```json
{
  "user": "Iris Doodle",
  "avatar": "images/team/iris.jpg",
  "timestamp": "Mar 19, 10:14 AM",
  "text": "Riley said casting Jerry as a gingerbread man 'felt wrong for about four seconds.' For the record, drawing him took forty minutes and at no point did it feel wrong. The icing mustache was the most natural thing I've ever put on a page. I don't know what that says about me."
```
